what_went_well: Thorough codebase exploration identified all 22 files in src/Telegram/, all consumers, and exact dependency chains
Spec covers all 6 subtasks with 38 scenarios, detailed file lists, and verification criteria
Dependency-driven execution order (5.1 -> 5.2 -> 5.5 -> 5.6 -> 5.3 -> 5.4) prevents broken intermediate states
Identified the need for ChannelManager.adminAction() method and RoleResolverInterface extraction — not in original task description
Phase 5 spec passes openspec validate with zero errors
what_went_wrong: Initial spec format used ### Task X.Y headers instead of ### Requirement: format required by openspec validator
Required multiple iterations to satisfy validator requirements (Requirement: prefix, requirement text, SHALL/MUST keywords)
improvement_suggestions: Pre-existing Phase 3 and Phase 4 specs also fail validation (different format) — consider fixing them for consistency
The TelegramRoleResolver concrete class stays in src/Telegram/Service/ but implements the new RoleResolverInterface — consider moving it to src/Channel/ in a future cleanup
TelegramBotRepository and TelegramChatRepository remain in src/Telegram/Repository/ — a future task could rename them to ChannelInstanceRepository and ChannelConversationRepository
deviations_from_spec: Spec notes TelegramRoleResolver stays as concrete implementation rather than being deleted — deliberate decision to avoid breaking role resolution while keeping the interface channel-agnostic
Spec keeps 2 repository files in src/Telegram/ rather than deleting the entire directory — admin UI still depends on them

metrics.validation_result: pass (phase5 only; phase3/phase4 have pre-existing format issues)
